data.narrative:
H Company operates in the AI & Software sector at Series D stage, having raised $220M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, ElevenLabs, Hive AI, and three others) and 1 public comparable (PLTR), all at equivalent stage or sector positioning.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($220M) as a proxy base value and scaling to $5.5B, applies a typical Series D forward multiplier of 1.5x, yielding $8.2B. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $3.8B; the peer set ranges from $1.0B (Hive AI) to $8.6B (Wayve), with middle quartile representation from Hugging Face ($4.5B) and ElevenLabs ($3.0B).

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $3.2B (low) to $9.5B (high), with a median of $6.0B. The wide dispersion—spanning stage-progression upside of $8.2B against peer-median anchor of $3.8B—reflects material uncertainty in comparable company selection and growth trajectory assumptions. Key risks include: peer set heterogeneity (Wayve operates in autonomous systems; Cohere in LLM APIs), limited public market anchoring, and early-stage revenue maturity across the comp set. The $6.0B median represents a defensible midpoint absent proprietary revenue or margin data.
